blocks.label: Simple Definition
blocks.text: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is the ability of a computer or machine to perform tasks that normally require human intelligence.

blocks.label: Technical Definition
blocks.text: AI is a branch of Computer Science that develops intelligent systems capable of learning, reasoning, problem-solving, decision-making, and understanding data.

blocks.heading: Why do we need AI? Because humans cannot always:
items: Process huge amounts of data quickly
Make instant decisions in every situation
blocks.note: AI helps automate these tasks efficiently.

blocks.heading: Real-Life Examples
items: Google Maps → Finds the best route

items.label: Normal Program
items.text: Follows fixed rules. Example: Calculator.

items.label: AI Program
items.text: Learns from data, improves over time. Example: ChatGPT.

blocks.heading: Key Points
items: AI can learn, reason, and make decisions.
AI is used in almost every industry today.

interviewQA.question: What is Artificial Intelligence?
interviewQA.answer: AI is the ability of machines to perform tasks that normally require human intelligence.

examQuestions.question: Explain Artificial Intelligence with suitable examples.

sections.heading: Goals of Artificial Intelligence
sections.intro: The goal of AI is to create machines that can perform tasks requiring human intelligence.

items.title: Learning
items.text: AI learns from data and improves over time.
items.example: Netflix recommends better movies based on your watching history.

items.title: Reasoning
items.text: AI uses logic to solve problems and make decisions.
items.example: Chess-playing AI chooses the best move.

items.title: Problem Solving
items.text: AI finds the best solution to a given problem.
items.example: Google Maps finds the shortest route.

items.title: Decision Making
items.text: AI selects the best option from multiple choices.
items.example: Banks use AI to approve or reject loans.

items.title: Perception
items.text: AI understands information from images, videos, speech, or sensors.

items.title: Automation
items.text: AI performs repetitive tasks automatically with minimal human intervention.
items.example: Customer support chatbots answer common questions.

blocks.heading: Key Points
items: AI aims to make machines intelligent.
AI learns from experience and data.
AI helps solve problems and make decisions.
AI is widely used in daily life.

interviewQA.question: What is the main goal of AI?
interviewQA.answer: To develop machines that can perform tasks requiring human intelligence.

interviewQA.question: Name any four goals of AI.
interviewQA.answer: Learning, Reasoning, Problem Solving, Decision Making.

examQuestions.question: What are the goals of Artificial Intelligence?
Explain the major goals of AI with examples.

blocks.heading: What is Human Intelligence?
blocks.note: Example: A teacher explains a concept in different ways until every student understands.

blocks.label: Artificial Intelligence
blocks.text: The ability of machines to perform tasks that normally require human intelligence. Example: ChatGPT answering questions or Google Maps finding the best route.

blocks.heading: Real-Life Example: Solving a Math Problem

items.label: 👨 Human
items.text: Understands the concept, uses logic, and may try different methods.

items.label: 🤖 AI
items.text: Solves it using patterns and algorithms learned from data.

blocks.heading: Key Points
items: AI is fast, but humans are creative.
AI has no real emotions or consciousness.
Humans can learn from limited experiences, while AI usually needs large amounts of data.

interviewQA.question: What is the main difference between AI and Human Intelligence?
interviewQA.answer: AI is man-made and data-driven, while Human Intelligence is natural, emotional, creative, and capable of common-sense reasoning.

interviewQA.question: Can AI completely replace humans?
interviewQA.answer: No. AI can automate many tasks, but it cannot fully replace human creativity, emotions, ethics, and common sense.

examQuestions.question: Differentiate between AI and Human Intelligence.
Compare AI and Human Intelligence with suitable examples.

blocks.label: Branches of AI
blocks.text: Different fields that focus on solving specific types of intelligent problems.

items.title: Machine Learning (ML)
items.text: Machines learn from data without being explicitly programmed.

items.title: Deep Learning (DL)
items.text: A subset of Machine Learning that uses artificial neural networks to solve complex problems.

items.title: Natural Language Processing (NLP)
items.text: Enables computers to understand, process, and generate human language.

items.title: Computer Vision
items.text: Allows computers to understand and analyze images and videos.

items.title: Robotics
items.text: Combines AI with machines to perform physical tasks automatically.

items.title: Expert Systems
items.text: AI systems that make decisions like a human expert using predefined knowledge and rules.

blocks.heading: Easy Trick to Remember
blocks.note: Expert Systems is also an important branch — remember it separately.

blocks.heading: Key Points
items: Each branch solves different types of problems.
Most modern AI applications combine multiple branches.

interviewQA.question: Name any four branches of AI.
interviewQA.answer: Machine Learning, Deep Learning, Natural Language Processing, and Computer Vision.

interviewQA.question: Which branch is used in ChatGPT?
interviewQA.answer: Natural Language Processing (NLP) along with Machine Learning and Deep Learning.

examQuestions.question: What are the branches of AI?
Explain the major branches of AI with examples.

blocks.label: AI Workflow
blocks.text: The step-by-step process of building an AI system, from identifying a problem to deploying the AI model.

items.title: Problem Definition
items.text: Identify the problem to solve.
items.example: Predict whether a student will pass or fail.

items.title: Data Collection
items.text: Collect relevant data.

items.title: Data Preprocessing
items.text: Clean and prepare the data — remove duplicate data, handle missing values, correct errors.

items.title: Model Training
items.text: Train the AI model using the prepared data.
items.example: The model learns the relationship between attendance and exam results.

items.title: Model Evaluation
items.text: Check how well the model performs.
items.example: If the model predicts correctly for 95 out of 100 students, its accuracy is 95%.

items.title: Deployment
items.text: Make the trained model available for real users.
items.example: Integrate it into a school management system.

items.title: Monitoring & Improvement
items.text: Continuously monitor performance and retrain if necessary.
items.example: Update the model with new student data every semester.

blocks.heading: Easy Trick to Remember
blocks.phrase: P D P T E D M

blocks.heading: Key Points
items: AI workflow is a sequence of steps to build an AI system.
Data quality directly affects AI performance.
AI models need continuous monitoring and improvement.

interviewQA.question: What is AI Workflow?
interviewQA.answer: AI Workflow is the complete process of developing an AI system, from problem definition to deployment and monitoring.

interviewQA.question: Why is data preprocessing important?
interviewQA.answer: It removes errors, missing values, and inconsistencies, improving the model's performance.

examQuestions.question: Explain the steps of AI Workflow with an example.

blocks.heading: What is Weak AI?
blocks.note: Weak AI (Narrow AI) is designed to perform one specific task. It cannot think or perform tasks outside its programming. Example: Google Maps can find the shortest route, but it cannot diagnose diseases or play cricket.

blocks.heading: What is Strong AI? (Hypothetical example — a robot that can:)
items: Make independent decisions like a human
blocks.note: Strong AI (General AI) is a theoretical AI that can perform any intellectual task a human can do. Strong AI does not exist yet — it is still a research goal.

blocks.heading: Weak AI vs Strong AI
rows: Can learn and reason like humans (theoretical)

items.label: 📱 Weak AI
items.text: Siri can answer questions but cannot become a doctor.

items.label: 🤖 Strong AI
items.text: An AI that could work as a doctor, teacher, engineer, and scientist — all in one system.

blocks.heading: Key Points
items: Weak AI is also called Narrow AI.
Almost all AI systems today are Weak AI.
Strong AI is still under development and has not been achieved.

interviewQA.question: What is Weak AI?
interviewQA.answer: Weak AI is AI designed to perform a specific task, such as voice assistants or recommendation systems.

interviewQA.question: Does Strong AI exist today?
interviewQA.answer: No. Strong AI is still a theoretical concept and has not been developed.

examQuestions.question: Differentiate between Weak AI and Strong AI.
Explain Weak AI and Strong AI with suitable examples.
